decks.notes: This deck focuses on using a combination of disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out, to control the game and keep the opponent's threats in check. It also utilizes efficient creatures like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf to apply pressure and close out the game quickly. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong board presence, ultimately outpacing the opponent and securing a victory through a combination of tempo plays and aggressive threats.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently play aggressive creatures and spells in order to put pressure on the opponent and win the game as fast as possible. The deck focuses on using cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler, Delver of Secrets, and Murktide Regent to quickly deal damage to the opponent, while also disrupting their plans with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. The deck also includes powerful removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat to handle opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to outpace and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of quick and impactful plays.

The key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Hexing Squelcher while disrupting their game plan with cards like Force of Will and Daze. By playing efficient spells like Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat, the deck aims to control the board and finish the game quickly with Murktide Regent. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, utilizing cards like Wasteland and fetch lands to ensure smooth gameplay. Overall, the deck focuses on applying pressure early and maintaining momentum throughout the game.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on utilizing cheap spells and creatures to control the board and deal damage quickly. It runs a high number of cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to find threats and answers efficiently. Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent provide early pressure, while Thundering Falls and Cori-Steel Cutter offer additional reach. Wasteland and Boomerang Basics disrupt opponent's mana base, while Lightning Bolt provides efficient removal. The deck also includes countermagic like Daze and Force of Will to protect its threats. Overall, the deck aims to win the game through aggressive and disruptive plays.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while applying pressure with efficient creatures. The deck uses cheap removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to interact with the opponent's board while playing disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to control the game. The deck also includes powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Brazen Borrower to quickly close out the game once the opponent's resources are depleted. Overall, this deck aims to control the game while applying enough pressure to efficiently close out victories.

The deck focuses on utilizing lands as a control mechanism, with key cards like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to create powerful tokens. It also includes strong land-based disruption like Wasteland and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale. The deck aims to maintain board control while also generating value through cards like Life from the Loam and Exploration. Additionally, it includes a toolbox of lands like Bojuka Bog and Boseiju, Who Endures to deal with various threats from the opponent. Ultimately, the deck seeks to outvalue and outlast the opponent through clever land manipulation and control.

The deck focuses on utilizing cycling creatures like Architects of Will and Street Wraith to fill the graveyard, then casting Living End to bring them back onto the battlefield for a powerful combo. This strategy is supported by cards like Shardless Agent and Violent Outburst to cascade into Living End, while also using disruptive cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect the combo. The deck also includes high-impact creatures like Colossal Skyturtle and Waker of Waves to pressure opponents and close out games quickly.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly apply pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Murktide Regent, while disrupting their game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. The deck also focuses on mana denial with Wasteland and control cards like Brazen Borrower and Hexing Squelcher. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat for removal, ensuring that the opponent's board stays clear for consistent attacks. This deck aims to win the game quickly and efficiently.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly and efficiently cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The deck is able to generate a lot of mana with cards like Lotus Petal and City of Traitors to enable these combos. Additionally, cards like Omniscience allow you to play spells for free, while Stock Up and Brainstorm help you dig for your combo pieces. The deck also includes counter magic like Force of Will to protect your combo and disrupt your opponent's game plan.

The deck aims to quickly cycle through cards to fill the graveyard, bringing out key pieces such as Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to dump the entire library into the graveyard. This triggers cards like Narcomoeba and Bridge from Below, allowing for a lethal Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return finish. The deck relies heavily on fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide to power out its combo quickly, while also utilizing Pact of Negation and Thoughtseize for protection and disruption. The goal is to win the game before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The main focus of the deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying threats quickly to apply pressure. It utilizes a combination of counter spells, discard spells, and efficient creatures to control the game and tempo. By utilizing powerful disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, along with versatile creatures like Brazen Borrower and Baleful Strix, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while presenting a clock with aggressive threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent. Wasteland is used to disrupt the opponent's mana base and slow them down, allowing the deck to maintain its aggressive edge.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo pieces needed to win the game with Thassa's Oracle. It uses cards like Doomsday, Personal Tutor, and Predict to set up the perfect pile of cards to win the game in a single turn. The deck also includes disruption in the form of counterspells like Force of Will and Daze, as well as hand disruption with cards like Thoughtseize. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and protect them long enough to win the game with Thassa's Oracle.

The main concept of this deck is to generate a large amount of mana quickly through cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, and then use that mana to combo off and win the game in a single turn. This is achieved by playing cards like Infernal Tutor and Burning Wish to find key pieces like Tendrils of Agony or Ad Nauseam. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Duress and Thoughtseize to protect the combo. Additionally, cards like Wishclaw Talisman and Urza's Saga provide additional ways to tutor for important combo pieces.

This combo deck aims to quickly dump its entire library into the graveyard using cards like Boggart Harbinger and Undercity Informer, to then win the game with Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return. By utilizing fast mana in the form of Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, as well as protection from cards like Pact of Negation and Cabal Therapy, the deck aims to win the game before the opponent has a chance to respond. The inclusion of cards like Once Upon a Time and Elvish Spirit Guide help increase consistency and speed to execute the combo effectively.

The deck aims to quickly mill through the entire library, utilizing cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer, to eventually win the game with Thassa's Oracle. By casting a flurry of free spells and rituals, the deck can achieve a fast and explosive win condition through a game-winning combo. The inclusion of Pact of Negation and Cabal Therapy also allows for protection and disruption, while cards like Memory's Journey and Bridge from Below can provide additional utility and redundancy to ensure the combo's success.

The deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Barrowgoyf to quickly put pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of Jegantha as a companion provides additional mana ramp and flexibility. With cards like Thoughtseize and Mutated Cultist for disruption and removal, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. The use of Urza's Saga and powerful creatures like Nethergoyf and Pyrogoyf further enhances the deck's aggressive strategy, making it a formidable force in the Legacy format.
